Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prelim Injunction
Off Calendar Aug-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANDREW B. KAY OF COZEN & O'CONNOR, TEL. NO. (202) 912-4864 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ANDREW D. LANPHERE OF PILLSBURY LLP, TEL. NO. 983-1321 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. STEVEN S. ROSENTHAL , ATTY. MARC COHEN & ATTY. ALICIA CLOUGH OF KAYE/SCHOLER LLP, TEL. NO. (202)682-3553 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS JOHN CHIANG AND THE OFFICE OF THE STATE CONTROLLER's Motion For Preliminary Injunction IS Denied. Defendants (the "Controller") have not sufficiently established a likelihood of success on the merits or irreparable harm. Although the evidence establishes that the Controller may audit Plaintiff ("Thrivent") pursuant to CCP sec. 1571(a), the Controller does not provide sufficient evidence establishing that the current audit is limited to the inspection of "reasonably relevant" information. (Brovelli v. Sup. Ct. (1961) 56 Cal.2d 524, 529.) Further, the Controller has not established that it or the People of the State of California will be irreparably harmed if a preliminary injunction is not issued. The denial of the motion for preliminary injunction will not prevent the Controller from ever performing an audit of Thrivent. Rather, the audit will be delayed until Thrivent's Constitutional arguments are resolved at trial. The Controller has not made a sufficient showing of irreparable harm by delaying the determination of these issues until after a trial on the merits. The court to look at the proposed order submitted by the prevailing party. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
